content.content: {"result":"[{\"name_path\": \"loadRunData\", \"kind\": \"Function\", \"relative_path\": \"autotest/framework/comparator.ts\", \"body_location\": {\"start_line\": 40, \"end_line\": 77}, \"body\": \"function loadRunData(\\n  resultsDir: string\\n): [config: any, summary: ResultsSummary, modelResults: Record<string, TestEntry[]>] {\\n  const config = readJson(join(resultsDir, \\\"config.json\\\")) ?? {};\\n  const summary: ResultsSummary = readJson(join(resultsDir, \\\"results-summary.json\\\")) ?? { runs: [] };\\n\\n  // Build modelResults from summary.runs (no tree walk needed)\\n  const modelResults: Record<string, TestEntry[]> = {};\\n\\n  for (const run of summary.runs ?? []) {\\n    // Derive model_slug from model string (same tr '/@:' '_' logic as runner-base.sh)\\n    const modelSlug = run.model.replace(/[\\\\/@:]/g, \\\"_\\\");\\n\\n    const entry: TestEntry = {\\n      test_id: run.test_id,\\n      model_slug: modelSlug,\\n      model: run.model,\\n      duration_seconds: run.duration_seconds,\\n      exit_code: run.exit_code,\\n      // Fields now available from enriched RunEntry:\\n      total_tokens: run.total_tokens ?? 0,\\n      prompt_tokens: run.prompt_tokens ?? 0,\\n      completion_tokens: run.completion_tokens ?? 0,\\n      total_turns: run.turns ?? 0,\\n      total_retries: run.retries ?? 0,\\n      time_to_first_tool_ms: run.time_to_first_tool_ms ?? null,\\n      api_time_ms: undefined, // Not in RunEntry; wall_time_ms approximates\\n      wall_time_ms: run.wall_time_ms ?? (run.duration_seconds * 1000),\\n      cost_usd: run.cost_usd ?? null,\\n      unique_tools: run.unique_tools ?? [],\\n    };\\n\\n    if (!modelResults[modelSlug]) modelResults[modelSlug] = [];\\n    modelResults[modelSlug].push(entry);\\n  }\\n\\n  return [config, summary, modelResults];\\n}\"}]"}

(run.duration_seconds * 1000),\\n      cost_usd: run.cost_usd ?? null,\\n      unique_tools: run.unique_tools ?? [],\\n    };\\n\\n    if (!modelResults[modelSlug]) modelResults[modelSlug] = [];\\n    modelResults[modelSlug].push(entry);\\n  }\\n\\n  return [config, summary, modelResults];\\n}\"}]"}

content.content: {"result":"[{\"name_path\": \"buildComparison\", \"kind\": \"Function\", \"relative_path\": \"autotest/framework/comparator.ts\", \"body_location\": {\"start_line\": 79, \"end_line\": 223}, \"body\": \"function buildComparison(\\n  config: any,\\n  summary: ResultsSummary,\\n  modelResults: Record<string, TestEntry[]>\\n): ModelComparison {\\n  const models = Object.keys(modelResults).sort();\\n  if (models.length === 0) {\\n    return {\\n      error: \\\"No model results found\\\",\\n      run_id: \\\"unknown\\\",\\n      suite: \\\"unknown\\\",\\n      models: [],\\n      test_cases: 0,\\n      comparison: [],\\n      aggregate_stats: {},\\n    };\\n  }\\n\\n  // All test IDs\\n  const allTestIds = [\\n    ...new Set(\\n      Object.values(modelResults)\\n        .flat()\\n        .map((e) => e.test_id)\\n    ),\\n  ].sort();\\n\\n  // Result map from summary\\n  const resultMap = new Map<string, string>();\\n  for (const run of summary.runs ?? []) {\\n    resultMap.set(`${run.test_id}::${run.model}`, run.result ?? \\\"UNKNOWN\\\");\\n  }\\n\\n  // Per-test comparison\\n  const comparison: ComparisonEntry[] = allTestIds.map((testId) => {\\n    const testEntry: ComparisonEntry = { test_id: testId, results: {}, winner: {} };\\n\\n    for (const modelSlug of models) {\\n      const entries = (modelResults[modelSlug] ?? []).filter(\\n        (e) => e.test_id === testId\\n      );\\n      if (entries.length === 0) continue;\\n\\n      const entry = entries[0];\\n      const modelName = entry.model;\\n      const resultKey = `${testId}::${modelName}`;\\n      const result = resultMap.get(resultKey) ?? \\\"UNKNOWN\\\";\\n      const passed = result.startsWith(\\\"PASS\\\")\\n        ? true\\n        : result === \\\"UNKNOWN\\\"\\n          ? entry.exit_code === 0\\n          : false;\\n\\n      testEntry.results[modelSlug] = {\\n        passed,\\n        result,\\n        duration_ms:\\n          entry.wall_time_ms || entry.duration_seconds * 1000,\\n        api_time_ms: entry.api_time_ms ?? 0,\\n        tokens: entry.total_tokens ?? 0,\\n        prompt_tokens: entry.prompt_tokens ?? 0,\\n        completion_tokens: entry.completion_tokens ?? 0,\\n        turns: entry.total_turns ?? 0,\\n        retries: entry.total_retries ?? 0,\\n        cost_usd: entry.cost_usd ?? null,\\n        tools: entry.unique_tools ?? [],\\n      };\\n    }\\n\\n    // Winners (among passing models)\\n    const passing = Object.entries(testEntry.results).filter(\\n      ([, r]) => r.passed\\n    ) as [string, any][];\\n    const winners: Record<string, string> = {};\\n    if (passing.length > 0) {\\n      winners.speed = passing.reduce((a, b) =>\\n        a[1].duration_ms <= b[1].duration_ms ? a : b\\n      )[0];\\n      winners.cost = passing.reduce((a, b) =>\\n        (a[1].cost_usd ?? Infinity) <= (b[1].cost_usd ?? Infinity) ? a : b\\n      )[0];\\n      winners.tokens = passing.reduce((a, b) =>\\n        a[1].tokens <= b[1].tokens ? a : b\\n      )[0];\\n    }\\n    testEntry.winner = winners;\\n\\n    return testEntry;\\n  });\\n\\n  // Aggregate stats per model\\n  const aggregate: Record<string, AggregateStats> = {};\\n  for (const modelSlug of models) {\\n    const entries = modelResults[modelSlug] ?? [];\\n    if (entries.length === 0) continue;\\n\\n    const modelName = entries[0].model;\\n    const durations = entries.map(\\n      (e) => e.wall_time_ms || e.duration_seconds * 1000\\n    );\\n    const tokens = entries.map((e) => e.total_tokens ?? 0);\\n    const costs = entries\\n      .map((e) => e.cost_usd)\\n      .filter((c): c is number => c != null);\\n    const turns = entries.map((e) => e.total_turns ?? 0);\\n    const retries = entries.map((e) => e.total_retries ?? 0);\\n\\n    let passCount = 0;\\n    let totalCount = 0;\\n    for (const testId of allTestIds) {\\n      const key = `${testId}::${modelName}`;\\n      const r = resultMap.get(key);\\n      if (r) {\\n        totalCount++;\\n        if (r.startsWith(\\\"PASS\\\")) passCount++;\\n      }\\n    }\\n\\n    const avg = (arr: number[]) =>\\n      arr.length > 0 ? arr.reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0) / arr.length : 0;\\n    const sum = (arr: number[]) => arr.reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0);\\n\\n    aggregate[modelSlug] = {\\n      model: modelName,\\n      tests_run: entries.length,\\n      pass_rate: totalCount > 0 ? Math.round((passCount / totalCount) * 1000) / 1000 : 0,\\n      avg_duration_ms: Math.round(avg(durations)),\\n      avg_tokens: Math.round(avg(tokens)),\\n      total_tokens: sum(tokens),\\n      avg_cost_usd: costs.length > 0 ? Math.round(avg(costs) * 1e6) / 1e6 : null,\\n      total_cost_usd: costs.length > 0 ? Math.round(sum(costs) * 1e6) / 1e6 : null,\\n      avg_turns: turns.length > 0 ? Math.round(avg(turns) * 10) / 10 : 0,\\n      total_retries: sum(retries),\\n    };\\n  }\\n\\n  return {\\n    run_id: config.run_id ?? \\\"unknown\\\",\\n    suite: config.suite ?? \\\"unknown\\\",\\n    models: models.map((m) => aggregate[m]?.model ?? m),\\n    test_cases: allTestIds.length,\\n    comparison,\\n    aggregate_stats: aggregate,\\n  };\\n}\"}]"}

content.text:
`★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────`
- Serena's `find_referencing_symbols` returned empty here because TypeScript cross-file references require LSP index warm-up — falling back to `search_for_pattern` found all 3 consuming files reliably.
- `ResultsSummary` is a **data transfer object** written by `aggregator.ts` and consumed by `comparator.ts` and any suite-specific analyze-results scripts. It's the boundary between test execution and analysis.
`─────────────────────────────────────────────────`

## 1. Where `ResultsSummary` is Defined

**`autotest/framework/types.ts`, lines 97–110**

## 2. All Fields and Their Types

```typescript
export interface ResultsSummary {
  runs: RunEntry[];
  summary: {
    total: number;
    passed: number;
    failed: number;
    errors: number;
    pass_rate: number;
    agent_distribution: Record<string, number>;
  };
  by_model: Record<string, { passed: number; failed: number; error: number }>;
  suite: string;
  models: string[];
}
```

| Field | Type | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `runs` | `RunEntry[]` | Raw per-run data (the source of truth) |
| `summary` | inline object | Aggregate counts + agent distribution map |
| `summary.total` | `number` | |
| `summary.passed` | `number` | |
| `summary.failed` | `number` | |
| `summary.errors` | `number` | |
| `summary.pass_rate` | `number` | 0–1 float |
| `summary.agent_distribution` | `Record<string, number>` | agent name → count |
| `by_model` | `Record<string, { passed; failed; error }>` | Note: `error` (no `s`) vs `errors` in summary |
| `suite` | `string` | Test suite name |
| `models` | `string[]` | Model IDs that ran |

---

## 3. Functions/Modules That Consume `ResultsSummary`

| File | Usage |
|---|---|
| `autotest/framework/aggregator.ts` (line 213) | **Produces** it — constructs the `ResultsSummary` object and writes `results-summary.json` |
| `autotest/framework/comparator.ts` (lines 43–45) | **Reads** it via `loadRunData()` — deserializes from `results-summary.json` |
| `autotest/framework/comparator.ts` (line 82) | **Passes** it into `buildComparison()` — iterates `summary.runs` to build result maps |

---

## 4. Files to Update If You Add a New Field

| File | Why |
|---|---|
| `autotest/framework/types.ts` | The interface definition itself |
| `autotest/framework/aggregator.ts` | Constructs the object literal at line 213 — must populate the new field |
| `autotest/framework/comparator.ts` | Reads the deserialized JSON — may need to use the new field in `loadRunData` or `buildComparison` |
| Any suite-specific `analyze-results.ts` (e.g. `autotest/tech-writer/analyze-results.ts`) | If they import and destructure `ResultsSummary`, they'd need updates too — the git status shows this file is currently modified |

The key risk is `comparator.ts` line 45: it deserializes from JSON with `?? { runs: [] }` as a fallback, so **old result files on disk won't have the new field** — you'll need a nullish default there.
